methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Glyphic Biotechnologies is a Series A biotech company founded by Joshua Yang and Daniel Estandian that has raised $44M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, including firms such as Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and Profluent at comparable or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods were applicable. Stage-Progression applied a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $44M raised proxy, implying $88M. Peer-Median analysis identified 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comps with a median valuation of $1.3B, range $100M to $3.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring using a 6.1x P/S median across biotech tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) could not be applied due to absent revenue disclosure. The three methods produced a triangulated range of $75M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $694M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $75M to $1.5B, with significant dispersion reflecting the heterogeneity of peer-stage trajectories and the absence of revenue data. Key uncertainties include the company's progress on key technical milestones since Series A close, burn rate relative to raised capital, and competitive positioning within the peer set. The wide range underscores reliance on peer multiples rather than company-specific fundamentals.
